strategic_objective: Investigate the stability and contextual sensitivity of class-conditioned variations in backward packet length features to understand their discriminative reliability.
key_checks: Test variation stability across different time windows and network conditions
Analyze sensitivity to attack-specific packet size manipulation or fragmentation
Identify confounding factors such as flow duration or application type influencing variations
success_criteria: Confirmation that class-conditioned variations persist under varying conditions
Evidence that variations are not confounded by unrelated flow or application factors
Characterization of feature behavior supporting discriminative use without shortcut risk
router_constraints: preserve evidence refs e9 to e12
